data.content:
I've reviewed all 5 project files. Here's my analysis of the issues and recommended improvements:

---

## Current Issues Found

### 1. Massive Property Duplication (all 5 projects)
Every project repeats these 8 properties identically:
`TargetFramework`, `LangVersion`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors`

### 2. Package Version Drift (no central management)
Several packages have inconsistent versions across projects:

| Package | Project A | Project B |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | **8.0.0** (Api, Core) | **8.0.1** (Services) |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** (Core.Tests) | **17.9.0** (Api.Tests) |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** (Core.Tests) | **2.7.1** (Api.Tests) |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** (Core.Tests) | **2.5.6** (Api.Tests) |

### 3. Duplicated Custom Target
The `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted across all 3 `src/` projects.

### 4. Duplicated DocumentationFile Setting
`<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>` is in all 3 `src/` projects.

### 5. Duplicated Test Boilerplate
Both test projects repeat `IsPackable` and the same 4 test package references.

### 6. No SDK Pinning
No `global.json` — builds may behave differently across machines.

---

## Recommended Solution

### A. Root `Directory.Build.props` — eliminate shared properties

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### B. `src/Directory.Build.props` — src-specific settings

```xml
<!-- /src/Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### C. `src/Directory.Build.targets` — shared custom target

```xml
<!-- /src/Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

### D. `test/Directory.Build.props` — test boilerplate

```xml
<!-- /test/Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### E. Root `Directory.Packages.props` — central package management

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Packages.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

### F. Resulting slimmed-down `.csproj` files

After these changes, projects shrink dramatically. For example, `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` becomes:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And `ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

### G. Add `global.json` for SDK pinning

```json
{ "sdk": { "version": "8.0.300", "rollForward": "latestFeature" } }
```

---

## Summary

| Change | Impact |
|---|---|
| Root `Directory.Build.props` | Eliminates 8 duplicated properties × 5 projects |
| `src/Directory.Build.props` | Centralizes `DocumentationFile` |
| `src/Directory.Build.targets` | Eliminates 3 copies of `PrintBuildInfo` |
| `test/Directory.Build.props` | Centralizes `IsPackable` for tests |
| `Directory.Packages.props` | Fixes 4 version mismatches, single source of truth for all versions |
| `global.json` | Pins SDK for reproducible builds |

Want me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **No `Directory.Build.props`** — All 5 projects duplicate these properties:
   - `TargetFramework` (net8.0)
   - `LangVersion` (latest)
   - `Nullable` (enable)
   - `ImplicitUsings` (enable)
   - `TreatWarningsAsErrors` (true)
   - `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` (true)
   - `Company` (Contoso)
   - `Authors` (Contoso Engineering)
   
2. **No Central Package Management** — Package versions are scattered across projects with inconsistencies: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` ranges from 8.0.0 to 8.0.1, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from 17.9.0 to 17.10.0, and `xunit` from 2.7.1 to 2.8.1 across different test projects.

3. **Duplicated custom build target and properties** — The `PrintBuildInfo` target and `DocumentationFile` property are copy-pasted across multiple projects instead of being centralized in shared `.targets` and `Directory.Build.props` files.

4. **Test project duplication** — Both test projects repeat `IsPackable` settings and test package references with varying versions, which could be consolidated in a `test/Directory.Build.props`, and there's no `global.json` to pin the SDK version.
